pr.title: Import symbol names and scopes from `GALE01.map` to `symbols.txt`
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Imported symbol names and scope metadata from the CodeWarrior `GALE01.map` data into `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, replacing many anonymous `fn_`, `lbl_`, and similar placeholders with map-derived names and adding `scope:global` or existing scope data where it was missing. The tooling in `tools/config-dtk` was adjusted so it parses the existing `symbols.txt`, selectively fills unnamed symbols and missing scopes from map tree symbols, serializes symbols back to text, and tolerates zero map addresses/alignments by using plain integer types instead of `NonZero` wrappers. The PR body only says it progresses #1082; there were no review comments in the dump.

key_files.role: Mass symbol table update: roughly 19k lines rewritten with map-derived names and explicit scopes while preserving addresses, sections, sizes, and other attributes.
Added symbol helpers such as `is_named`, attribute serialization, and `to_txt`; simplified parsing to return a `BTreeMap<u32, Symbol>`.
Changed config-dtk flow to read existing `symbols.txt`, merge names/scopes from parsed map symbols, and write updated symbol text back.
Changed table symbol address and alignment fields from `NonZeroU32`/`NonZeroU8` to plain `u32`/`u8`, removing hard errors on zero values.

what_changed: Renamed many previously anonymous symbols using names from `GALE01.map`; examples in the diff include `fn_80003268` to `TRK_memset`, `fn_8000CE50` to `expf`, `fn_8000CEE0` to `powf`, and many `fn_800xxxxx` entries to subsystem-prefixed names such as `lbShadow_`, `lbArq_`, `lbMemory_`, `lbHeap_`, `lbArchive_`, and `lbDvd_`.
Added explicit scope annotations to many symbol lines, especially `scope:global`, while retaining existing cases like `scope:local` for symbols such as `__init_registers`.
Preserved multiple symbols at the same address where applicable; the excerpt shows `fn_800051CC` and `gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd` both at `.init:0x800051CC`, with ordering adjusted.
Added `Symbol::is_named`, treating names beginning with `fn_`, `lbl_`, or `func_` as unnamed placeholders for import purposes.

Added `Symbol::to_txt` and `write_attr` to serialize parsed config symbols back into the canonical `symbols.txt` line format, including optional `type`, `size`, `scope`, `align`, `data`, and `hidden` attributes.
Changed the merge behavior so map-derived names are only assigned when an existing symbol is not considered named, and map-derived scopes are only assigned when the existing symbol has no scope.
Simplified `dtk_config` parsing from a `Parser` struct with a `symbols` field to a `parse_symbols` function returning a `BTreeMap<u32, Symbol>`.
Changed `cw_map::TableSymbol` address and alignment storage from nonzero wrapper types to plain integer types, removing errors for zero address or zero alignment values.
smart_moves: The importer did not blindly replace every existing symbol name: it only overwrote placeholder-style names starting with `fn_`, `lbl_`, or `func_`, reducing the risk of clobbering manually curated names.
Scope import was similarly conservative: it filled `scope` only when the existing config symbol lacked one.
Serializing through `Symbol::to_txt` kept output formatting centralized instead of duplicating line formatting in `main.rs`.
Using `BTreeMap<u32, Symbol>` kept output ordered by address, which is important for a searchable symbol table and for minimizing noisy reordering.
The existing/retained size-filling logic computes missing zero-size map table entries from the next sorted address before use, which makes imported size metadata more useful.
Relaxing `cw_map` parsing to accept zero address/alignment values made the import tool more tolerant of real map-file edge cases instead of failing the whole import.
decomp_lessons: When importing external map metadata, treat the map as a source for missing or placeholder information, not necessarily as authority over all existing curated decomp names.
Define an explicit placeholder-name test such as `fn_`, `lbl_`, and `func_` before bulk renaming; this makes mass symbol imports reproducible and reviewable.
For large generated config updates, keep serializer logic in a dedicated method so future imports preserve formatting and optional attributes consistently.

Preserve and enrich existing `symbols.txt` records rather than regenerating from only one source; this avoids losing attributes such as type, size, align, data, hidden flags, or manually assigned scopes.
Map-file parsers should tolerate odd but parseable metadata such as zero alignments or addresses when the downstream merge can still make progress.
Bulk symbol-table changes are valuable decomp infrastructure: better names and scopes improve searchability, cross-reference readability, and future matching work even without changing C source.
naming_conventions: Anonymous function placeholders use address-based names like `fn_8000CE50`; anonymous labels use `lbl_...`; `func_...` is also treated as a placeholder prefix by the tool.
Map-derived library names may use known runtime names directly, such as `TRK_memset`, `expf`, and `powf`.

Unidentified functions in known subsystems can be given subsystem prefixes plus address suffixes, as shown by `lbShadow_8000E9F0`, `lbArq_80014ABC`, `lbMemory_80014EEC`, `lbHeap_800158D0`, `lbArchive_80016AF0`, and `lbDvd_800174E8`.
Existing decomp-style library names such as `lbVector_Normalize`, `lbVector_WorldToScreen`, and `lbArchive_InitializeDAT` coexist with address-suffixed placeholders for still-unknown functions.
Symbol lines follow `name = .section:0xADDR; // type:<kind> size:0xSIZE scope:<scope>` with optional `align`, `data`, and `hidden` attributes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `GALE01.map` as external evidence to rename and scope symbols before or alongside decompilation, improving future assembly comparisons and cross-reference navigation.
For map symbols with size zero, infer a size from the next symbol address after sorting symbols by address.
Keep address-based suffixes on subsystem-classified but not fully understood functions, so names communicate both likely module ownership and exact binary location.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR dump slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rely on map-derived symbol names from PR #1088.
Search for additional config-dtk changes after PR #1088 to see whether `Symbol::is_named` placeholder rules were expanded.
Inspect issue #1082 for the broader goal behind importing `GALE01.map` names and scopes.
Search for symbols still named `fn_`, `lbl_`, or `func_` after this import to identify remaining unnamed work.
Look for later PRs that convert address-suffixed subsystem names such as `lbDvd_80017AB0` into semantic function names.
